content.text:
[Sun 2026-04-19 16:00 UTC] Shape Sort in `repo/` is too hard to use on phones because the drag targets are too small.

Use the git checkout in `repo/` to get this hotfix onto `review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix` and push that review branch to the local `origin`.

content.thinking:
The user wants me to:
1. Work in the `repo/` directory
2. Create a hotfix branch for Shape Sort mobile usability (drag targets too small)
3. Branch name: `review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix`
4. Push to local `origin`

Let me start by exploring the repo structure and understanding the codebase, then make appropriate changes to fix the mobile drag target size issue.

arguments.command: cd repo && git status && git log --oneline -3

content.thinking: Let me read these files to understand the drag target implementation.

content.text:
<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
  <head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>Shape Sort</title>
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="style.css">
  </head>
  <body>
    <main class="shape-sort-board"></main>
    <script src="game.js"></script>
  </body>
</html>

content.thinking:
I can see the issue. The drag targets are too small on mobile. The current implementation has:
- `tileSizePx: 72` - the visual tile size
- `dragPaddingPx: 6` - only 6px of padding around tiles for grabbing

This makes the effective grab area 72 + 6*2 = 84px, but on mobile that's still quite small for touch targets. Mobile UX guidelines typically recommend at least 44x44px touch targets, but ideally larger for better usability.

The fix should increase `dragPaddingPx` to make the drag targets easier to hit on mobile. Let me create the review branch and apply the fix.

content.text: Now I understand the issue. The `dragPaddingPx: 6` creates a very small grab area around the 72px tiles. On mobile, this is hard to hit. I'll increase the padding to make drag targets more forgiving.

content.thinking: Now I'll apply the fix to increase the drag padding for better mobile usability.
The fix is applied. Now I need to commit and push this to the local origin.

arguments.command:
cd repo && git add public/games/shape-sort/game.js && git commit -m "fix(shape-sort): increase drag padding for mobile usability

Increase dragPaddingPx from 6 to 18 pixels to expand touch targets.
Makes tiles easier to grab on phone screens where 6px padding
created frustratingly small hit areas around 72px tiles." && git push origin review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix

content.text:

content.thinking: Done! The hotfix branch has been created and pushed. Let me verify the changes.

content.text:
Done. The hotfix branch `review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix` is now pushed to origin.

**Change made:** Increased `dragPaddingPx` from 6 → 18 pixels in `public/games/shape-sort/game.js`.

This triples the invisible grab area around each tile (from 84px to 108px effective hit target), making Shape Sort much more usable on phones without changing the visual tile size.
